summary: Jeevan HealthCare at Home: full enterprise-grade AI-powered home healthcare super app with React Native frontend, Node.js/Express/MongoDB backend, role-based dashboards (Patient, Doctor, Nurse, Corporate HR, Admin, Super Admin), 7 core modules (Home Healthcare, Preventive/Corporate, Digital Health, Mother & Child, Wellness, Specialist Care, B2B), AI symptom checker embedded in Health tab, Razorpay payments, Firebase notifications, WhatsApp API, seeded sample data, web admin panel, and an enterprise-grade Doctor Consultation / Specialist Booking system with 16 specialty categories, end-to-end booking flow (slot selection, patient details, payment, confirmation, reminders, video consultation, post-consultation, reviews), and admin panel for doctor/slot/revenue management.
architectural_notes: Symptom Checker is an embedded component inside the Health Tab (Digital Health Module), NOT a standalone route. No /symptom-checker entry in root layout.
Tech stack: React Native (mobile), Node.js + Express + MongoDB (backend), JWT + RBAC auth, Firebase push notifications, Razorpay payments, WhatsApp Business API.
6 user roles: Patient, Doctor, Nurse/Caregiver, Corporate HR, Admin, Super Admin — each with separate dashboard and permission-based access.
Bottom tab navigator: Home | Bookings | Health | Subscriptions | Profile.
Modular feature-based folder structure; each module (Healthcare, Corporate, Wellness, Specialist, Digital Health, etc.) is independent with separated backend routes and controllers.
Doctor Consultation module uses 16 specialty category groups with expandable UI; booking supports Online, Clinic Visit, Home Visit, Follow-up, Corporate, and Chronic Care Subscription modes.
Slot locking: selected appointment slots are locked for 5 minutes with a countdown; auto-released if payment is not completed.
known_issues: Text visibility issue reported — text may not be rendering visibly due to color/contrast problems.

existing_features.title: Update the global theme so that any UI currently using the Jeevan green/teal brand color is rendered in Medical Blue instead, across all pages and dashboards.

Ensure Tailwind theme tokens and utilities remain consistent after the color change by updating the CSS variables used by Tailwind (e.g., `--secondary`, `--accent`, related chart tokens) so charts/badges/status elements no longer use green.

Update the footer brand logo element (selected by XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/footer[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/img[1]) to use the user-uploaded Jeevan HealthCare logo image instead of the current src (/assets/generated/jeevan-logo-transparent.dim_200x200.png). Add the chosen uploaded image file to the frontend static assets and update only this element in frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx accordingly (src and, if needed to prevent distortion, its sizing classes).

Remove any standalone /symptom-checker route entry from the root router configuration (e.g., App.tsx or equivalent main router file) to eliminate the missing-route console warning. The Symptom Checker must not appear as a separate top-level route or stack screen.

Ensure the Symptom Checker component continues to function correctly as an embedded component inside the Health tab's Digital Health section. It must not be accessible via a direct URL or navigation link outside of the Health tab.

Remove all orphaned imports, navigation links, and redirect logic that reference the removed /symptom-checker route across the entire frontend codebase (e.g., any useNavigate('/symptom-checker'), <Link to='/symptom-checker'>, or similar references in header, sidebar, or other navigation components).

Fix the text visibility of the second button element located at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/div[1]/section[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/button[2] — this is the second CTA button in the first section of the main page. Ensure its label text is clearly legible by applying sufficient color contrast between the button text and its background. The fix must be consistent with the current Medical Blue theme.

Inside the existing Doctor Consultation section, render the full specialty category list as clean, vertically stacked expandable accordion groups. Each group header must display its emoji icon and category name. When expanded, the group reveals its list of specialist types as selectable items. The 16 groups are: 🩺 Primary Care (General Physician, Pediatrician, Gynecologist, Geriatrician, Family Medicine), ❤️ Cardiac & Vascular (Cardiologist, Interventional Cardiologist, Cardiothoracic Surgeon, Vascular Surgeon), 🧠 Neuro Sciences (Neurologist, Neurosurgeon, Neuropsychiatrist, Neuro Physiotherapist), 🫁 Respiratory (Pulmonologist, TB & Chest Specialist, Critical Care Specialist), 🦴 Orthopedic & Rehab (Orthopedic Specialist, Joint Replacement Surgeon, Spine Specialist, Sports Medicine, Physiotherapist), 🧴 Skin & Aesthetics (Dermatologist, Cosmetologist, Trichologist, Aesthetic Specialist), 🩸 Endocrine & Metabolic (Diabetologist, Endocrinologist, Obesity Specialist), 🩻 Digestive & Liver (Gastroenterologist, Hepatologist, Endoscopy Specialist), 🩺 Kidney & Urinary (Nephrologist, Urologist, Andrologist), 👁 Eye Care (Ophthalmologist, Retina Specialist, Cataract Surgeon), 👂 ENT & Hearing (ENT Specialist, Audiologist, Rhinologist), 🧠 Mental Health (Psychiatrist, Clinical Psychologist, Counsellor), 🧬 Cancer Care (Medical Oncologist, Surgical Oncologist, Radiation Oncologist), 👶 Pediatric Super Specialists (Pediatric Cardiologist, Pediatric Neurologist, Pediatric Pulmonologist, Pediatric Endocrinologist), 🤰 Women & Fertility (Fertility Specialist, Reproductive Endocrinologist, High-Risk Pregnancy Specialist), 💊 Pain & Emergency (Pain Management Specialist, Palliative Care Specialist, Emergency Medicine). Only one group may be expanded at a time (single-open accordion). Styling must follow the existing Medical Blue theme.

Create a dedicated 'Consult a Doctor' page (or expand the existing DoctorConsultationPage) with a prominent search bar at the top that accepts free-text queries for doctor name, specialty, or disease/condition. As the user types, display matching suggestions grouped by type (Doctors, Specialties, Conditions). Styling must follow the existing Medical Blue theme.

Below the search bar on the 'Consult a Doctor' screen, add a horizontal scrollable filter chip row with the following filter options: Online, Home Visit, Near Me, Fee (opens a fee-range selector), and Experience (opens an experience-range selector). Only one primary mode filter (Online / Home Visit / Near Me) may be active at a time; Fee and Experience filters are independently toggleable. Active filter chips must be visually distinct using the Medical Blue theme.

Below the filter chips, display the 16 specialty category tiles/cards in a responsive grid (2 columns on mobile, 4 columns on wider screens). Each tile must show the category emoji icon and category name. Tapping a category tile navigates the user to or scrolls to a filtered doctor listing for that specialty. The category grid must reuse the data already defined in the existing SpecialtyAccordion component (the same 16 groups) so there is a single source of truth. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

Below the specialty category grid, render a doctor listing section that shows mock doctor cards. Each doctor card must display: doctor photo (avatar placeholder if no image), full name, specialty, years of experience, star rating, consultation fee, and available consultation modes (Online / Clinic / Home Visit) as small badge icons. When a filter is active (from REQ-10) or a category is selected (from REQ-11), the displayed doctor cards must be filtered accordingly. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

Update the mock doctor card data and the DoctorConsultationPage doctor listing section so that each doctor card displays all of the following fields: doctor photo (avatar placeholder if no image), full name, qualification (e.g., MBBS, MD), specialty, years of experience, star rating (numeric + star icons), starting consultation fee, available consultation modes (Online / Clinic / Home Visit) as small badge icons, and a prominent 'Book Now' button styled in the Medical Blue theme. Ensure the card layout is clean, readable, and consistent across all viewport sizes.

Add a sorting control above the doctor listing section on the DoctorConsultationPage. The control must offer four sort options: 'Highest Rated', 'Lowest Fee', 'Most Experienced', and 'Available Today'. The active sort option must be visually highlighted. When a sort option is selected, the doctor card list must re-order accordingly in real time without a page reload. 'Available Today' must filter the list to only show doctors whose mock availability includes the current day. Styling must follow the existing Medical Blue theme.

Create a dedicated Doctor Listing Screen (route: `/doctor-listing/:specialty`) that displays doctor cards filtered by the selected specialty. The screen must show a heading reflecting the selected specialty name (e.g., 'Cardiologists' or the specialty passed as a route param). The list of doctor cards must be filtered from the existing mock doctor data in DoctorConsultationPage to only show doctors matching the selected specialty. Reuse the existing mock doctor dataset already present in DoctorConsultationPage.tsx as the single source of truth.

Each doctor card on the Doctor Listing Screen must display: a doctor photo (avatar/placeholder if no image), full name, qualification (e.g., MBBS, MD), years of experience, star rating (numeric value + star icons), starting consultation fee, and a prominent 'Book Now' button styled in the Medical Blue theme. Card layout must be clean, readable, and consistent across all viewport sizes.

Clicking the 'Book Now' button on any doctor card in the Doctor Listing Screen must navigate the user to a Doctor Profile screen (route: `/doctor-profile/:doctorId`). The Doctor Profile screen must display the full details of the selected doctor: photo, name, qualification, specialty, experience, rating, consultation fee, available consultation modes (Online / Clinic / Home Visit) as badge icons, and a brief bio/description. The profile screen must also include a prominent 'Book Appointment' button at the bottom, styled in the Medical Blue theme.

In `frontend/src/components/Header.tsx`, locate the `<strong>` element inside the top bar's phone/contact span (XPath: /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/header[1]/div[1]/span[1]/strong[1]) and replace its text content with '+91 9700104108'.

Update the Doctor Listing Screen (`frontend/src/pages/DoctorListingPage.tsx`) so that each doctor card displays all of the following fields: doctor photo (avatar/placeholder if no image), full name, qualification (e.g., MBBS, MD), specialty, years of experience, star rating (numeric value + star icons), starting consultation fee, available consultation modes (Online / Clinic / Home Visit) as small badge icons, and a prominent 'Book Now' button styled in the Medical Blue theme. The card layout must be clean, readable, and consistent across all viewport sizes. Reuse the shared mock doctor dataset from `frontend/src/lib/mockDoctors.ts` as the single source of truth.

Add a sorting control above the doctor card list on the Doctor Listing Screen (`frontend/src/pages/DoctorListingPage.tsx`). The control must offer four sort options: 'Highest Rated', 'Lowest Fee', 'Most Experienced', and 'Available Today'. The active sort option must be visually highlighted using the Medical Blue theme. When a sort option is selected, the doctor card list must re-order in real time without a page reload. 'Available Today' must filter the list to only show doctors whose mock availability includes the current day of the week.

Create a 'Choose Consultation Type' screen (route: `/consultation-type/:doctorId`) that is navigated to when the user clicks 'Book Appointment' on the Doctor Profile screen. The screen must display three selectable consultation type cards: 💻 Online, 🏥 Clinic Visit, and 🏠 Home Visit. Only one type may be selected at a time. Each card must display the consultation type label, its icon, and a brief description (e.g., 'Video call with doctor', 'Visit doctor at clinic', 'Doctor visits your home'). Styling must follow the existing Medical Blue theme.

On the 'Choose Consultation Type' screen, implement automatic fee calculation logic: (1) When 'Home Visit' is selected, add a travel fee (mock value: ₹200) to the base consultation fee and display it as a line item labelled 'Travel Fee'. (2) When any type is selected, check for and apply a corporate discount (mock: 10% off) if the user has a corporate account, displaying the discount as a line item labelled 'Corporate Discount'. (3) Check for and apply a subscription discount (mock: 15% off) if the user has an active subscription, displaying it as a line item labelled 'Subscription Discount'. Display a fee breakdown summary below the type cards showing: Base Fee, Travel Fee (Home Visit only), Corporate Discount (if applicable), Subscription Discount (if applicable), and Total Payable. Discounts should be applied sequentially on the discounted price. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

Add a prominent 'Continue' button at the bottom of the 'Choose Consultation Type' screen, styled in the Medical Blue theme. The button must be disabled until a consultation type is selected. On click, it should navigate the user to the next step in the booking flow (slot selection page at `/book-slot/:doctorId` or equivalent). Pass the selected consultation type and calculated total fee as route/query parameters to the next screen.

Update the 'Book Appointment' button on the Doctor Profile screen (`frontend/src/pages/PublicDoctorProfilePage.tsx`) to navigate to the new 'Choose Consultation Type' screen (`/consultation-type/:doctorId`) instead of any previous destination, passing the doctorId as a route parameter.

Create a 'Select Date & Time' slot selection page at route `/book-slot/:doctorId`. The page must receive the `doctorId`, selected consultation type, and total fee as route/query parameters passed from the 'Choose Consultation Type' screen (`/consultation-type/:doctorId`). Register this route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` following the existing TanStack Router pattern used for other booking-flow routes.

On the 'Select Date & Time' page, render an interactive calendar view at the top of the screen. The calendar must display the current month with selectable dates. Past dates must be visually disabled and non-selectable. The currently selected date must be highlighted using the Medical Blue theme. The user must be able to navigate to the next/previous month.

Below the calendar on the 'Select Date & Time' page, display the available time slots for the selected date in 30-minute intervals (e.g., 09:00 AM, 09:30 AM, 10:00 AM, etc.). Use mock data to populate available and unavailable slots. Available slots must be displayed as selectable cards/buttons; unavailable (booked) slots must be visually distinct and non-selectable. Slots must span a reasonable clinic day (e.g., 09:00 AM – 07:00 PM). Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

Implement slot locking logic on the 'Select Date & Time' page: when a user selects an available slot, that slot must be immediately marked as 'locked' in local component state and a 5-minute countdown timer must be displayed prominently near the slot or in a summary bar (e.g., 'Slot held for 04:32'). If the user does not complete payment (i.e., does not proceed to the next step) within 5 minutes, the slot lock must expire, the slot must return to 'available' state, and the countdown display must be cleared. The timer must be implemented using a client-side countdown (e.g., `setInterval` / `useEffect`). Only one slot may be locked at a time; selecting a different slot resets the timer.

Add a 'Confirm Slot' button at the bottom of the 'Select Date & Time' page, styled in the Medical Blue theme. The button must be disabled until both a date and a time slot are selected and the slot lock timer is active. On click, it must navigate the user to the next step in the booking flow (patient details / payment page). Pass the selected date, time slot, doctorId, consultation type, and total fee as route/query parameters to the next screen.

Create a 'Patient Details' page at route `/patient-details` that receives `doctorId`, `consultationType`, `totalFee`, `date`, and `timeSlot` as route/query parameters passed from the 'Select Date & Time' page (`/book-slot/:doctorId`). Register this route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` following the existing TanStack Router pattern used for other booking-flow routes.

At the top of the Patient Details page, render a patient type selector with three mutually exclusive options: 'Self', 'Family Member', and 'Corporate Employee'. Only one option may be selected at a time. The selected option must be visually highlighted using the Medical Blue theme.

Below the patient type selector on the Patient Details page, render a form that collects the following fields: Full Name (text input), Age (numeric input), Gender (radio group or select: Male / Female / Other), Phone (numeric input, 10 digits), Symptoms (multi-line text area). All fields must be required. Styling must follow the existing Medical Blue theme.

When the selected consultation type (received as a query parameter) is 'Home Visit', conditionally show an Address field (multi-line text area) in the Patient Details form between the Phone and Symptoms fields. The Address field must be required when visible. When the consultation type is not 'Home Visit', the Address field must be hidden and not required.

Below the Symptoms field on the Patient Details page, add an 'Upload Reports' file input that allows the user to upload one or more files (accept common document and image formats: PDF, JPG, PNG). Display the names of selected files below the input. The upload field is optional. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

Add a prominent 'Continue' button at the bottom of the Patient Details page, styled in the Medical Blue theme. The button must be disabled until: (a) a patient type is selected, and (b) all required form fields (Name, Age, Gender, Phone, Symptoms, and Address if Home Visit) are filled. On click, navigate the user to the next step in the booking flow (payment page or booking confirmation page), passing all collected patient details along with doctorId, consultationType, totalFee, date, and timeSlot as route/query parameters.

Create a 'Payment' page at route `/payment` in `frontend/src/App.tsx` following the existing TanStack Router pattern. The page must receive `doctorId`, `consultationType`, `totalFee`, `date`, `timeSlot`, `patientName`, `patientAge`, `patientGender`, `patientPhone`, `patientType`, and `symptoms` as route/query parameters passed from the Patient Details page (`/patient-details`).

On the Payment page, render a fee breakdown summary section at the top that displays the following line items: (1) Consultation Fee — the base doctor consultation fee from mock data keyed by doctorId; (2) Travel Fee — shown only when consultationType is 'Home Visit' (mock value: ₹200); (3) GST — calculated as 18% of (Consultation Fee + Travel Fee, before discounts); (4) Discount — shown as a negative line item representing the total discount amount applied (corporate + subscription discounts from the previous step, passed via totalFee parameter); (5) Final Total — the grand total after all additions and deductions. All monetary values must be displayed in Indian Rupee format (₹). Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

On the Payment page, below the fee breakdown section, render a 'Select Payment Method' section offering five mutually exclusive payment method options: (1) 💳 UPI, (2) 💳 Card, (3) 🏦 Net Banking, (4) 👛 Wallet, (5) 🏠 Pay at Home. Each option must be displayed as a selectable card/tile with its icon and label. Only one payment method may be selected at a time. The currently selected method must be visually highlighted using the Medical Blue theme. 'Pay at Home' option must display a brief note such as 'Pay cash to the doctor at the time of visit'. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

On the Payment page, add a prominent 'Pay Now' button at the bottom styled in the Medical Blue theme. The button must be disabled until a payment method is selected. On click, simulate a successful payment (mock — no real payment gateway integration), generate a unique alphanumeric Booking ID (e.g., `JHC-XXXXXXXX` where X is a random alphanumeric character, generated client-side), and navigate the user to a Booking Confirmation screen at route `/booking-confirmation`, passing the Booking ID, doctorId, consultationType, totalFee, date, timeSlot, and patientName as route/query parameters.

Create a 'Booking Confirmation' page at route `/booking-confirmation` in `frontend/src/App.tsx`. The page must receive and display the Booking ID, doctor name (looked up from mock data via doctorId), consultation type, final total fee, appointment date, time slot, and patient name. The page must display a prominent success indicator (e.g., a green checkmark icon or Medical Blue success banner) with the message 'Booking Confirmed!' and show all booking details in a summary card. Include a 'Go to Home' button and a 'View My Bookings' button at the bottom, both styled in the Medical Blue theme. The Booking ID must be displayed prominently and clearly labelled.

Update the 'Continue' button on the Patient Details page (`frontend/src/pages/PatientDetailsPage.tsx`) to navigate to the new Payment page at route `/payment` (instead of any previous destination), passing all collected patient details along with `doctorId`, `consultationType`, `totalFee`, `date`, and `timeSlot` as route/query parameters.

Create a new frontend component `frontend/src/components/AppointmentReminders.tsx` that implements a client-side reminder system for booked appointments. The component must accept the appointment date/time, doctorId, and consultationType as props. It must schedule and display three in-app reminder notifications at exactly 24 hours, 3 hours, and 1 hour before the appointment time. Each reminder must display a banner/toast notification with a clear message (e.g., 'Your appointment is in 24 hours', 'Your appointment is in 3 hours', 'Your appointment is in 1 hour') and the doctor name and scheduled time. Reminders must be triggered using `setTimeout` / `useEffect` based on the time remaining until the appointment. Styling must follow the existing Medical Blue theme.

On the `AppointmentReminders` component (and on the Booking Confirmation page `frontend/src/pages/BookingConfirmationPage.tsx`), add a 'Join Video Call' button that is hidden and disabled until exactly 15 minutes before the appointment time. When the current time is within 15 minutes of the appointment start time, the button must become visible and enabled, displaying the label 'Join Video Call' with a video camera icon. The button must be styled prominently in the Medical Blue theme. For consultationType values other than 'Online', the button must not be rendered. The visibility transition must be driven by a client-side `useEffect`/`setInterval` countdown.

Integrate the `AppointmentReminders` component into `frontend/src/pages/BookingConfirmationPage.tsx`. Pass the appointment date, timeSlot, doctorId, and consultationType (already available as URL search params on that page) as props to the component. The reminders section must appear below the booking summary card and above the navigation buttons. A brief section header such as 'Appointment Reminders' must label this section.

Create a new page `frontend/src/pages/OnlineConsultationPage.tsx` at route `/online-consultation` that serves as the consultation execution screen for Online consultation type. The page must receive `doctorId`, `bookingId`, `date`, `timeSlot`, and `patientName` as URL search params. The screen must display a header with the doctor's name and specialty (looked up from mock data via doctorId), booking ID, and appointment time. Register this route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` following the existing TanStack Router pattern.

On the OnlineConsultationPage, render a Secure Video section as the primary content area. Display a mock video call interface with a large video placeholder area (showing the doctor's avatar and name), a patient self-view thumbnail (bottom-right corner), and a control bar at the bottom with the following toggle buttons: 🎤 Mute/Unmute microphone, 📷 Camera on/off, 📞 End Call (red, navigates back to Booking Confirmation or Home). The video area must show a 'Waiting for doctor to join…' status message before the call is active and 'Connected' once the mock call starts (auto-transition after 3 seconds). Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

On the OnlineConsultationPage, add a collapsible Chat panel (slide-in from the right or as a tab below the video area). The chat panel must display a message thread area (initially empty with a placeholder 'No messages yet'), a text input field at the bottom, and a Send button. Clicking Send appends the typed message to the thread as a patient message (right-aligned, Medical Blue background) in local component state. Mock doctor replies (left-aligned, grey background) must appear automatically 2 seconds after each patient message with a static response such as 'Thank you, I have noted that.' The chat toggle button must be accessible from the video control bar (💬 icon). Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

On the OnlineConsultationPage, add a File Upload section accessible via a 📎 icon button in the video control bar or chat panel. Clicking the icon opens a file picker (accept PDF, JPG, PNG). Selected files are listed below the chat input with their filename and size. Each uploaded file displays a remove (×) button. Files are stored in local component state only (no backend upload). A maximum of 5 files may be attached at once; exceeding the limit shows an inline error message. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

On the OnlineConsultationPage, add an e-Prescription section accessible via a 📋 tab or button (labelled 'e-Prescription'). The section must display a prescription form with the following read-only pre-filled fields: Patient Name, Doctor Name, Date; and the following editable fields: Diagnosis (text area), Medicines (a dynamic list where each entry has: medicine name text input, dosage text input, frequency select — Once/Twice/Thrice daily, duration text input, and a remove row button), and Additional Notes (text area). An 'Add Medicine' button appends a new empty medicine row. A 'Generate Prescription' button at the bottom renders the completed prescription as a styled summary card (mock PDF preview) and shows a 'Download PDF' button (which triggers a browser print dialog). Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

Create a new page `frontend/src/pages/HomeVisitTrackingPage.tsx` at route `/home-visit-tracking` that serves as the consultation execution screen for Home Visit consultation type. The page must receive `doctorId`, `bookingId`, `date`, `timeSlot`, and `patientName` as URL search params. The screen must display a header with the doctor's name, specialty, and booking ID. Register this route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` following the existing TanStack Router pattern. From the Booking Confirmation page (`frontend/src/pages/BookingConfirmationPage.tsx`), when `consultationType` is 'Home Visit', the 'Join / Track Visit' button (or equivalent CTA) must navigate to `/home-visit-tracking` with the appropriate query params.

On the HomeVisitTrackingPage, render a Live Tracking section as the primary content area. Display a mock map-style tracking UI consisting of: a rectangular placeholder area (styled with a subtle grid/map background) labelled 'Live Map', a doctor avatar marker icon on the map that animates (CSS transition) from one position to another every 5 seconds to simulate movement, a patient home marker icon at a fixed position, and a dashed route line between the two markers. Below the map, display a status banner that cycles through 'Doctor has accepted your request', 'Doctor is on the way', and 'Doctor is nearby' at 8-second intervals to simulate real-time status updates. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

On the HomeVisitTrackingPage, display an ETA (Estimated Time of Arrival) section prominently below the map. Show a large countdown timer that starts at a mock value of 18 minutes and counts down to 0, formatted as 'MM:SS'. Display a label 'Estimated Arrival' above the timer and a sub-label 'Doctor is on the way' below it. When the countdown reaches 0, replace the timer with the message 'Doctor has arrived!' styled as a success banner in green/Medical Blue. The countdown must be implemented using `setInterval` / `useEffect`. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

On the HomeVisitTrackingPage, add a 'Contact Doctor' section below the ETA section. It must display two action buttons: (1) 📞 Call Doctor — displays a modal/popover with the mock doctor phone number (e.g., '+91 98765 XXXXX') and a 'Close' button; (2) 💬 Message Doctor — opens an inline chat panel identical in behaviour to the chat panel described in REQ-47 (local state only, mock auto-reply after 2 seconds). Both buttons must be styled prominently in the Medical Blue theme. Additionally, display the doctor's name, photo/avatar, and a 'Verified Doctor' badge in this section.

Update `frontend/src/pages/BookingConfirmationPage.tsx` to add consultation execution entry-point buttons below the AppointmentReminders section and above the navigation buttons. For `consultationType === 'Online'`, display a 'Start Consultation' button (visible and enabled only when current time is within 15 minutes of the appointment, using the same `setInterval`/`useEffect` pattern already used for the 'Join Video Call' button) that navigates to `/online-consultation` with doctorId, bookingId, date, timeSlot, and patientName as query params. For `consultationType === 'Home Visit'`, display a 'Track Doctor' button (always visible once booking is confirmed) that navigates to `/home-visit-tracking` with the same query params. Both buttons must be styled in the Medical Blue theme.

Create a new page `frontend/src/pages/PostConsultationPage.tsx` at route `/post-consultation` in `frontend/src/App.tsx` following the existing TanStack Router pattern. The page must receive `doctorId`, `bookingId`, `consultationType`, `date`, `timeSlot`, and `patientName` as URL search params. The page must display a prominent header reading 'Post-Consultation' with a sub-header showing the doctor's name (looked up from mock data via doctorId) and the booking ID.

On the PostConsultationPage, render a 'Download Prescription' action card. The card must display a 📄 icon, the label 'Download Prescription', and a brief description such as 'Get your e-prescription issued during the consultation'. Clicking the card triggers a browser print dialog (window.print()) rendering a styled prescription summary that includes the doctor name, patient name, booking ID, date, and a placeholder diagnosis and medicine list. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

On the PostConsultationPage, render an 'Order Medicines' action card. The card must display a 💊 icon, the label 'Order Medicines', and a brief description such as 'Order prescribed medicines for home delivery'. Clicking the card navigates the user to the existing Tests/Medicines catalog page (route `/tests` or equivalent already present in the app). Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

On the PostConsultationPage, render a 'Book Lab Tests' action card. The card must display a 🧪 icon, the label 'Book Lab Tests', and a brief description such as 'Schedule diagnostic tests recommended by your doctor'. Clicking the card navigates the user to the existing tests catalog page (route `/tests` or equivalent already present in the app). Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

On the PostConsultationPage, render a 'Schedule Follow-up' action card. The card must display a 📅 icon, the label 'Schedule Follow-up', and a brief description such as 'Book a follow-up appointment with the same doctor'. Clicking the card navigates the user to the 'Choose Consultation Type' screen at route `/consultation-type/:doctorId`, passing the same `doctorId` from the current page's URL params so the booking flow restarts for the same doctor. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

On the PostConsultationPage, arrange the four action cards (Download Prescription, Order Medicines, Book Lab Tests, Schedule Follow-up) in a responsive 2-column grid on mobile and a 2×2 or 4-column grid on wider screens. Each card must have a consistent height, a hover/active highlight state using the Medical Blue palette, and clear icon + label + description layout. Include a 'Go to Home' button at the bottom of the page styled in the Medical Blue theme that navigates to `/`.

Update `frontend/src/pages/BookingConfirmationPage.tsx` to add a 'Post-Consultation' navigation entry point. After the consultation execution buttons (Start Consultation / Track Doctor), add a clearly labelled section or button titled 'Post-Consultation Actions'. This button must always be visible on the confirmation page and must navigate to `/post-consultation` passing `doctorId`, `bookingId`, `consultationType`, `date`, `timeSlot`, and `patientName` as query params. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.

Create a new page `frontend/src/pages/RatingReviewPage.tsx` at route `/rating-review` in `frontend/src/App.tsx` following the existing TanStack Router pattern. The page must receive `doctorId`, `bookingId`, `consultationType`, `date`, `timeSlot`, and `patientName` as URL search params. The page must display a prominent header reading 'Rate Your Consultation' with a sub-header showing the doctor's name (looked up from mock data via doctorId) and the booking ID.

On the RatingReviewPage, implement a delayed display trigger: the rating & review form must not be visible immediately. Instead, show a countdown or waiting state that resolves after a mock 2-hour delay simulated by an immediately-visible 'Review Unlocked' state for demo purposes (since a real 2-hour wait is impractical in a browser session). Display an informational banner reading 'Reviews can be submitted 2 hours after your consultation' and immediately show the form in an unlocked state so users can interact with it. Styling must follow the existing Medical Blue theme.

On the RatingReviewPage, render a star rating control labelled 'Rate Doctor'. The control must display 5 interactive star icons. The user must be able to select a rating from 1 to 5 stars by clicking or tapping a star. The selected rating must be visually highlighted (filled stars) in the Medical Blue palette. Hovering over stars must preview the rating before selection. The selected numeric rating value must be stored in local component state.

On the RatingReviewPage, render a multi-line text area labelled 'Write Your Review' for the user to enter free-text feedback about their consultation. The text area must have a placeholder such as 'Share your experience with the doctor…'. Below the text area, display a character counter (e.g., '0 / 500') limiting input to 500 characters. The entered review text must be stored in local component state. Styling must follow the existing Medical Blue theme.

On the RatingReviewPage, render a 'Would you recommend this doctor?' section labelled 'Recommend?' with two mutually exclusive toggle buttons: '👍 Yes' and '👎 No'. Only one option may be selected at a time. The selected option must be visually highlighted using the Medical Blue palette. The selected recommendation value must be stored in local component state. Styling must follow the existing Medical Blue theme.

On the RatingReviewPage, add a prominent 'Submit Review' button at the bottom styled in the Medical Blue theme. The button must be disabled until: (a) a star rating is selected, and (b) the review text area is not empty. On click, simulate a successful review submission (mock — store the review in local component state and display an inline success message such as 'Thank you! Your review has been submitted.'). After submission, disable all form fields and the Submit button to prevent re-submission. Include a 'Go to Home' button below the success message that navigates to `/`.

Update `frontend/src/pages/PostConsultationPage.tsx` to add a 'Rate & Review' action card in the existing 2-column action cards grid. The card must display a ⭐ icon, the label 'Rate & Review', and a brief description such as 'Share your feedback and rate your doctor'. Clicking the card navigates to `/rating-review` passing `doctorId`, `bookingId`, `consultationType`, `date`, `timeSlot`, and `patientName` as query params. The card must have consistent height, hover/active highlight state using the Medical Blue palette, and follow the same layout as the other action cards on the page.

Create a new static page file `frontend/src/pages/DatabaseSchemaPage.tsx` that displays the 10 core database tables — Users, Doctors, Specialties, Doctor_Schedules, Appointments, Payments, Reviews, Medical_Records, Corporate_Accounts, and Subscriptions — as individual cards arranged in a responsive grid layout. Each card must show the table name as a heading and list all field names (with their data types) for that table as plain text rows. The page must be a single, self-contained React functional component that exports a default component. It must contain no routing logic, no backend or API calls, no database connections, no state management (no useState, useReducer, or context), no external library imports beyond React itself and Tailwind CSS classes, no animations, no reusable sub-components, no dynamic rendering (no .map, conditional rendering, or computed values), no props, and no hooks (no useEffect, useState, useRef, etc.). All content must be written as hardcoded JSX. Styling must follow the existing Medical Blue theme using Tailwind utility classes consistent with the rest of the application.

Update `frontend/src/pages/DatabaseSchemaPage.tsx` so that each of the 10 table cards (Users, Doctors, Specialties, Doctor_Schedules, Appointments, Payments, Reviews, Medical_Records, Corporate_Accounts, Subscriptions) displays the following hardcoded content in order: (1) Table Name as a bold heading, (2) a one-line plain-English description of the table's purpose, (3) a field list where each row shows 'Column Name – Data Type', (4) the Primary Key field clearly labelled (e.g., with a 'PK' badge or inline label), and (5) any Foreign Key fields clearly labelled with a 'FK' badge or inline label indicating the referenced table. All content must remain hardcoded JSX — no .map(), no useState, no useEffect, no hooks, no external library imports beyond React and Tailwind CSS classes. The component must remain a single self-contained default-export functional component with no props, no routing logic, no backend calls, and no animations. Styling must follow the existing Medical Blue theme using Tailwind utility classes consistent with the rest of the application.

new_feature_requests.summary: Update platform theme: change green UI color to Medical Blue (site-wide branding update).
Replace an existing UI image/asset with a user-provided image in the deployed app.
Build full React Native mobile app with all 7 healthcare modules, role-based dashboards, booking flow, AI symptom checker (embedded in Health tab), payments, notifications, and seeded sample data.
Fix missing /symptom-checker route warning by removing standalone route from root layout; keep Symptom Checker embedded inside Health tab only.
Fix text visibility issue — ensure text is visible (likely contrast or color issue).

Add Doctor Consultation section with 16 expandable specialty categories (Primary Care, Cardiac, Neuro, Respiratory, Orthopedic, Skin, Endocrine, Digestive, Kidney, Eye, ENT, Mental Health, Cancer, Pediatric Super Specialists, Women & Fertility, Pain & Emergency) under the Specialist Care module.

Build end-to-end specialist booking flow: specialty selection → doctor listing (cards with photo/rating/fee/modes) → doctor profile → consultation mode (Online/Clinic/Home Visit) → slot calendar → patient details → payment (Razorpay/UPI/Card) → confirmation (SMS/WhatsApp/Email/push) → reminders → consultation execution (video SDK, live tracking) → post-consultation (e-Prescription, medicines, lab tests, follow-up) → rating & review.
Admin panel enhancements for Doctor Consultation: add/edit doctors, set fees and availability, manage slots, view revenue analytics, manage corporate accounts, subscriptions, and handle refunds.
Build 'Select Specialist' / 'Consult a Doctor' screen with search (by doctor/specialty/disease), filters (Online/Home/Near Me/Fee/Experience), and category selection.

Doctor listing cards must show Photo, Name, Qualification, Experience, Rating, Starting Fee, Available Modes, and a Book button. Add sorting options: Highest Rated, Lowest Fee, Most Experienced, Available Today.
Create Doctor Listing Screen for selected specialty: doctor cards with Photo, Name, Qualification, Experience, Rating, Starting Fee, and 'Book Now' button that navigates to Doctor Profile screen.
Replace existing phone number with +91 9700104108 in the app.

Build 'Choose Consultation Type' screen: user selects Online, Clinic Visit, or Home Visit. System automatically adds travel fee for Home Visit, applies corporate discount, and applies subscription discount.
Build 'Select Date & Time' slot selection screen: calendar view, available slots in 30-min intervals, slot lock for 5 minutes with countdown, auto-release if payment not completed.

Build 'Patient Details' screen in the booking flow: user selects patient type (Self / Family Member / Corporate Employee) and collects Name, Age, Gender, Phone, Address (if Home Visit), Symptoms, and Upload Reports.

Build Payment page in booking flow: show fee breakdown (Consultation Fee, Travel Fee, GST, Discount, Final Total), offer payment methods (UPI, Card, Net Banking, Wallet, Pay at Home), and generate a Booking ID upon successful payment.
Build Reminder System for appointments: send reminders at 24 hours, 3 hours, and 1 hour before appointment; enable 'Join Video Call' button 15 minutes prior to the appointment.
Build consultation execution features: Online consultation includes Secure Video, Chat, File Upload, and e-Prescription; Home Visit consultation includes Live Tracking, ETA, and Contact Doctor.
Build Post-Consultation screen: user can Download Prescription, Order Medicines, Book Lab Tests, and Schedule a Follow-up appointment.
Build Rating & Review screen: shown 2 hours after consultation, allows user to Rate Doctor (star rating), Write a Review (text), and indicate if they would Recommend the doctor.

Create a static Database Schema Viewer page showing the 10 core tables (Users, Doctors, Specialties, Doctor_Schedules, Appointments, Payments, Reviews, Medical_Records, Corporate_Accounts, Subscriptions) with their fields in clean cards — pure static UI, no routing/API/state/hooks/animations/external libs.

Update the Database Schema page to display all 10 tables as clean cards with: Table Name (bold heading), short 1-line description, field list (Column Name – Data Type), Primary Key label, and Foreign Key labels — all as hardcoded HTML/JSX, no dynamic rendering.
